scope: AgenC agents/fork-context.ts ↔ Codex V2 control.rs spawn_subagent fork-mode behavior. Locks ForkMode shape, last_n_turns slicing, None-mode semantics, and the I-36 parent-rollout flush before fork. AgenC directive-prompt composition is presentation text and must not alter fork-context behavior.

requiredBehaviors: ForkMode union mirrors codex SpawnAgentForkMode at codex-rs/core/src/agent/control.rs:47-49
two variants: { kind: 'full_history' } and { kind: 'last_n_turns'; n: number }
no extra variants: codex has no 'new'/'explicit' kinds (Phase B.6 stripped these for strict parity)
mode === undefined returns directive-only initial messages

matches codex Option<SpawnAgentForkMode>::None semantic at codex-rs/core/src/agent/control.rs:386 — the LastNTurns truncation only runs when fork_mode is Some(LastNTurns), and full_history is the only Some(_) path that copies parent history
no parent history is read when mode is undefined; child starts with task directive alone
last_n_turns mode delegates rollout slicing to truncateRolloutToLastNForkTurns
when no rollout store is available, the in-memory parentMessages array is sliced via lastNUserTurns instead (functional equivalent of codex's user-turn boundary slicing)
rolloutItemsToForkMessages walks rollout output through filterForkedRolloutItems before mapping to LLMMessage
matches codex's pre-fork filter pass that strips items the child must not inherit (commentary, half-emitted assistant tool-call records, response-item types not allowed in child input)
compacted rollout items use payload.replacementHistory (codex's compacted snapshot semantic)
forkSubagent calls parent.rolloutStore.flushDurable() before reading parent history (I-36)
best-effort: a flush failure must not block the fork — caller's I-8 emitError surfaces it

rationale: codex's RolloutRecorder::get_rollout_history at agent/control.rs:383-385 reads from a durable file the recorder owns; AgenC's batched recorder needs an explicit flush to give the child the same coherent snapshot

approvedExceptions.reason: Allows TypeScript top-type annotations and Codex not-recognized error assertions; behavior rows remain required.
